CHAPTER 13.
AN ACT relating to the office of sheriff , providing additional
duties , powers and compensation therefor ; repealing all parts
of Sections 3780 , 3781 , 3782 , 3783 , 3784 , 3785 and 3786 , Ken-
tucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 and 1936 Editions , except those
parts in each of said sections relating to patrols in counties
in this Commonwealth having cities of the first and second
classes ; and declaring that an emergency exists .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby created and established a
County Highway Patrol and Investigator for each
county for the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and the
duties thereof shall be performed by the qualified
and acting sheriff and his duly appointed and quali-
fied deputy sheriffs in each county .
§ 2. It shall hereafter be the duty of the sheriff
and his appointed and qualified deputies to patrol
each public road and highway in his county , and to
direct , regulate and control the traffic on said roads
and highways so as to maintain a maximum degree
of safety to the general public in the use thereof.
He shall , as soon as he can do so , after receiving in-

CHAPTER 13 117
formation of their occurrence , investigate all acci-
dents , collisions and wrecks occurring upon said
roads and highways , and , when possible , determine
the position of each of the vehicles connected there-
with immediately before and after each accident ,
collision or wreck , and make and record the measure-
ment and direction and location of all tracks , marks
and visible impressions made on and about the high-
way where accidents , collisions or wrecks have oc-
curred that appear to have been made by the
vehicles or either of them connected therewith , or by
the parties owning , operating or occupying said
vehicles at the time of the accident , collision or
wreck that have a tendency to disclose what was the
cause thereof , and to make a record in his office of
his said observations and findings . He shall also
ascertain , if possible , the license number of each of
the vehicles connected therewith , the number of each
engine and the make of each car , and the name of
the state , territory , district , department and county
issuing said license , and the name and address of
the owner or operator of said vehicle , and the name
and address of each occupant of said vehicles in said
accident , collision or wreck, and the name and ad-
dress of each witness thereof , and the name and ad-
dress of each person who immediately thereafter
came upon the ground or who saw evidence of the
position of the vehicles immediately before or after
the accident , collision or wreck , or who heard a state-
ment or statements as to how or who caused the ac-
cident , collision or wreck made by the parties who
owned , operated or occupied either of the vehicles
connected therewith . When any person is wounded "
or killed , or there is reason to believe that criminal
negligence or carelessness was the cause of the acci-
dent , collision or wreck, said officer making the in-

118 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
spection , shall take affidavits or statements from all
witnesses who have information of incriminating
facts connected therewith , and for the purpose of
securing said affidavits or statements , each sheriff
and his authorized deputy is authorized and empow-
ered , forthwith and upon his own initiative , to issue
and serve a subpoena upon said witnesses requiring
them to testify , and is hereby authorized and em-
powered to administer an oath to each of said wit-
nesses before testifying , and to reduce to writing
said testimony, and he shall return said affidavits
and statements , together with a report in writing of
all information required , to the county attorney of
his county and a duplicate of said report and state-
ments or affidavits to the circuit court clerk of his
county for submission to the foreman of the grand
jury next to be convened in said county .
§ 3. Said sheriff or his deputies shall visit , in-
spect and investigate the way and manner each
owner or operator of any public house , hall , pavil-
ion , ground or place where music is furnished or
permitted , or where public dancing is conducted or
permitted , and also all camps , restaurants , road-
houses , or any place where intoxicating liquors are
sold or furnished or permitted to be sold or fur-
nished to the public , or where men and women are
furnished rooms for lodging either in the day or the
night in said county at least once each month , and to
report in writing to the county attorney of his coun-
ty his conclusions , together with the name and ad-
dress of any witness who claims knowledge of any
violation of law at said place or places or of any dis-
turbance occurring at said place or places , and shall
also file with the circuit court clerk of his county a
duplicate of each of said reports , to be delivered to

CHAPTER 13 119
the foreman of the grand jury next to be convened
in said county.
§ 4. Each county sheriff for the additional serv-
ices herein required shall receive reasonable com-
pensation therefor , and not more than One Thou-
sand Five Hundred Dollars ( $1,500.00 ) , per annum ,
to be determined by the Auditor of Public Accounts
of the Commonwealth and paid by the treasurer
thereof out of the General Fund , provided the com-
pensation of said officer as sheriff , together with his
other compensation as sheriff , as now provided by
law , shall not in any year exceed a total of Five
Thousand Dollars ( $ 5,000.00 ) , together with the
reasonable compensation of his deputy sheriffs and
the expenses of his office as sheriff as now allowed
by law, said additional compensation to commerce
from the effective date of this Act .
Section Four -a (4-a ) . The Auditor in determin-
ing the amount to be paid shall pay to such sheriffs
that sum of money which will not cause the sheriff
to draw in excess of the largest sum heretofore re-
ceived by the sheriff during the tenure of his office
or of his predecessor and in no event in excess of
the Constitutional limit .
§ 5. All parts of Sections 3780 , 3781 , 3782 , 3783 ,
3784 , 3785 , and 3786 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
1930 and 1936 Editions , except those parts in each of
said sections relating to patrols in counties in this
Commonwealth having cities of the first and second
class are hereby repealed .
6. Nothing in this Act shall be construed to
affect or impair the duties or obligations of County
Patrols in counties having a city of the first or
second class , State Highway Patrols , Field Repre-
sentatives of the Division of Motor Transportation
or any other officer of the state , county , city or town ,

120 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
not mentioned herein , irrespective of whether or not
enumerated herein or similar to those enumerated
herein , that are not by the terms of this Act
abolished .
§ 7. Whereas , The protection of the health ,
safety and morals of the community require the im-
mediate performance of the additional services
herein required of each sheriff and his deputies in
each county of Kentucky , an emergency is hereby
declared to exist , and this Act shall become a law
and be effective on its passage by the General As-
sembly and its approval by the Governor .
Approved January 18 , 1937 .

CHAPTER 15 .
AN ACT relating to certain officers and employees of the De-
partment of Public Health and the Department of Public Wel-

CHAPTER 15 121
fare of cities of the first class and providing for the appoint-
ment and compensation of certain members of the Depart-
ment of Public Health and Department of Public Welfare ; for
the creation of a personnel commission of four appointed
members with the Mayor as a member ex officio , with power
to vote , and the appointment , qualification , terms of office ,
removability and compensation of the appointed members of
the said commission ; for the levying and assessment of taxes
to support the said commission and defining the rights , pow-
ers and duties of said commission , and providing for the or-
ganization and administration of the said departments and
making the violation of the provisions of this act a misde-
meanor and providing penalties for the violation thereof and
fixing the time when the changes provided for herein shall
become effective ; providing for disciplinary measures appli-
cable to the enforcement of this act and providing for a merit
system of employment in the Department of Public Welfare
and Public Health in cities of the first class ; providing for
a method of extending the provisions of this act to other de-
partments and agencies of cities of the first class by alder-
manic action ; making the provisions hereof effective on other
departments and agencies of the said cities when extended
by aldermanic action ; defining certain terms used in this act ;
providing a method of extending the provisions of this act
to include additional positions in the Department of Public
Health and Department of Public Welfare ; prohibiting the
making or soliciting of political contributions by the em-
ployees in positions subject to the provisions of this act ; pro-
viding for maintaining the validity of the remainder of the
act in case any part shall be declared invalid , and providing
for the repeal of any acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1. Definitions . The term " emergency ap-
pointment ," as used in this act , shall be held to
mean any appointment to any position subject to the
provisions of this act, which may be made only in
the absence of an appropriate eligible list for the
position and which may be made without competi-
tive examination pending the establishment of an
eligible list and which appointment shall not exceed

122 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the duration of the emergency and in no case to ex-
ceed ninety days in any one fiscal year .
The term " probationary appointment , " as used
in this act , shall be held to mean an appointment to
any position subject to the provisions of this act ,
which shall be made in accordance with the pro-
visions of Section 5 of this act and which shall not
be less than six months nor more than one year in
duration .
The term " regular appointment , " as used in this
act , shall be held to mean an appointment to a posi-
tion subject to the provisions of this act made after
an employee has served a probationary period in a
manner deemed satisfactory to his department head .
The term " personal commission , " as used in this
act , shall be held to mean the agency established by
this act , including the governing body of five person-
nel commissioners , the personnel director , and such
other assistants as may be employed by such agency
in carrying out the provisions of this act .
The term " personnel commissioner , " as used in
this act , shall be held to mean a member of the gov-
erning body of the personnel commission as pro-
vided for in Section 2 of this act .
The term " personnel director , " as used in this
act , shall be held to mean the chief administrative
officer of the personnel commission as provided for
in Section 3 of this act .
The term " appointing authority , " as used in this
act , shall be held to mean the department or agency
head having the power of appointment or removal
from positions as provided for in this act .
The term " competitive examination , " as used in
this act , shall include consideration and rating of
any or all of the following qualifications of appli-
cants ; education , training , experience , general adap-

CHAPTER 15 123
tability , special aptitude , physical fitness , knowl-
edge , skill , personality , character and such other
qualifications as may be deemed necessary for the
satisfactory performance of the duties of the respec-
tive positions .
The term " elegible list , " as used in this act , shall
be held to mean a list of names of persons who have
been found qualified through suitable competitive
examinations for positions or classes of positions as
provided for in this act .
The term " service ratings ," as used in this act ,
shall be held to mean the evaluation of the efficiency
and general worth of employees in positions subject
to the provisions of this act as determined by the
department or agency head in the department or
agency in which the employee is employed .
The term " dismissal , " as used in this act , shall be
held to mean the discharge of the employee by the
department or agency head .
The term " lay-off, " as used in this act , shall be
held to mean the separation of an employee from the
service because of a reduction of force owing to de-
creased work or decreased funds in the department
or agency in which the employee has been employed .
The term " class " or " class of positions , " as used
in this act , shall be held to mean a position or group
of positions subject to the provisions of this act suf-
ficiently similar in duties , responsibilities and quali-
fication requirements to be designated by the same
title and placed within the same salary range .
The term " reinstatement , " as used in this act ,
shall be held to mean the re - appointment of a person
who formerly held a position , subject to the pro-
visions of this act , to such former position or to any
other position in the same class .
The term " transfer , " as used in this act , shall be

124 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
held to mean a change by an employee from one
position to another position within the same class .
The term " promotion , " as used in this act , shall
be held to mean a change from a position in a lower
class to a position in a higher class involving an in-
crease in responsibility or a change in title .
The term " demotion , " as used in this act , shall be
held to mean a change from a position in a higher
class to a position in a lower class involving a de-
crease in responsibility or a change in title .
The term " suspension , " as used in this act , shall
be held to mean the separation of an employee from
the service for a temporary or fixed period of time ,
which separation is inflicted upon the employee by
his appointing authority , as a disciplinary measure
under the rules established by the personnel
commission .
§ 2. Appointment , Qualification , Compensation ,
Tenure , Removal and Meetings of the Personnel
Commissioners . There shall be created and estab-
lished in cities of the first class a personnel commis-
sion to be governed by a board of five commission-
ers , said board to be composed of two members to be
appointed by the Board of Trustees of the largest
municipal university located in the said city, two
members to be appointed by the Board of Education
of said city and the Mayor of the said city shall be a
member of the said commission ex officio . Each
personnel commissioner appointed hereunder shall
serve for a term of four years except for those first
appointed under this act , which shall be for terms of
one, two , three and four years respectively . The
Board of Trustees of the largest municipal univer-
sity of said city shall appoint those personnel com-
missioners whose terms expire at the end of the first
and third years respectively and shall appoint all

CHAPTER 15 125
successors to such personnel commissioners . The
Board of Education of said city shall appoint those
personnel commissioners whose terms expire at the
end of the second and fourth years and shall appoint
all successors to such personnel commissioners .
Provided further , however , any personnel com-
missioner appointed to fill the vacancy for any un-
expired term shall be appointed only for the remain-
der of such unexpired term. Any personnel com-
missioner appointed according to the provisions of
this act shall be eligible for reappointment . A per-
sonnel commissioner may be removed during his
term of office by the authority by whom he was
appointed .
Members of the personnel commission shall be at
least twenty-five years of age and shall have been
residents and voters in the county in which said city
is located for at least three years and no member of
the said board shall be eligible for any appointive
or elective office of the said city during the time he
serves as a member of the said board . Each per
sonnel commissioner may be paid a salary not to
exceed a per diem allowance of Ten Dollars for each
meeting attended . Provided , however , that the an-
nual allowance of any one commissioner shall not
exceed Three Hundred ( $300.00 ) Dollars .
The personnel commissioners shall hold at least
one regular meeting each month and may hold such
additional meetings as may be required .
§ 3. Personnel Director- Appointment , Duties
and Compensation . The personnel commissioners
shall employ a personnel director , which personnel
director shall be a person thoroughly in sympathy
with the merit system and with the administration
of personnel , and skilled therein , and shall be a per-
son of recognized character and ability .

126 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
The personnel director may be removed only for
cause and after public hearing by the personnel com-
missioners and after at least ten days notice shall
have been given to the said director , which notice
shall contain a copy of charges preferred against
him .
The compensation of said personnel director shall
be fixed by the personnel commissioners and ap-
proved by the legislative body of said city . The
personnel director shall set up and direct the actual
work of the personnel commission and perform such
other functions as may be required of him by the
personnel commissioners and shall appoint such as-
sistants to the administrative staff of the personnel
commission as may be necessary to carry out the
provisions of this act .
§ 4. Rules to be Promulgated ; Reports by Com-
mission; Classification of Positions ; Competitive
Examinations ; Selection of Employees ; Promotions .
The personnel commission shall, after public notice
and hearing , make , promulgate and , when necessary ,
amend rules for the appointments , transfer , laying-
off , reinstatement , promotion , demotion , discharge
and suspension of all employees of the personnel
commission and of the Department of Public Health
and Public Welfare ( excepting , however , the Direc-
tor of Public Health and his private secretary , the
Director of Public Welfare and his private secre-
tary , resident medical staff , University visiting staff
and student nurses ) which employees hold positions
requiring technical training , special experiences and
administrative responsibilities as set forth in this
act , or included under its provisions by appropriate
action of the Board of Aldermen of such cities ; and
for the classification of such employees for appoint-
ment , promotion and transfer and shall report its

CHAPTER 15 127
proceedings and rules to the legislative body , the
Mayor and the Director of Public Welfare and the
Director of Public Health . Such rules shall , among
other things , provide :
(a) For the standardization and classification
of such positions and employments in said depart-
ments , agencies and commission , which positions
and employments require technical training , special
experience or administrative responsibilities and
the classification thereof into groups and subdivi-
sions to be based upon and created according to the
respective duties and responsibilities thereof and
shall be so arranged as to promote the filling of the
higher grade as far as practicable by promotion .
The salary range shall be uniform as to the same
standardization or classification ;
(b) For open competitive tests to ascertain the
relative fitness of all applicants for appointment to
said positions or employments in the said depart-
ments , agencies and commissions , and such tests
shall be practicable and relate to matters which will
fairly measure the relative fitness of candidates to
discharge the duties of the positions to which they
seek appointment . Notice of such tests shall be
given not less than ten days in advance by public
advertisement in at least one newspaper of general
circulation in the city and by posting notices in the
City Hall ;
(c) For the creation of eligible lists upon which
shall be entered the names of successful candidates
in the order of their standing through examination ;
and , for the filling of places in said departments ,
agencies and commission by the heads thereof who
shall select from not more than three candidates
graded highest on such eligible list , which list shall
remain in force not longer than one year . In the
absence of any appropriate eligible list any position
1

128 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
may be filled temporarily by the head of the depart-
ment , agency or commission in which such position
is to be filled , without examination , for a period not
to exceed ninety days , during which time the per-
sonnel commission shall hold the necessary exami-
nation for the filling of the place regularly , but per-
sons may be temporarily employed for emergency
work without examination for the period of the
emergency , in no event to exceed ninety days in any
one fiscal year , such appointment to be an emer-
gency appointment and the same shall not be re-
newed . No person shall be appointed or employed
under any title not appropriate to the duties to be
performed and no person shall be transferred to or
assigned to perform any duties of any position sub-
ject to competitive tests unless he shall have been
appointed to the position from which transfer is
made as a result of an open competitive test equi-
valent to that required for the position to be filled .
Each list of eligibles with the respective grades
shall be open to public inspection . Any person ap-
pointed from the eligible list and laid off for lack of
work or lack of appropriation shall be placed at the
head of the eligible list and shall be eligible for re-
appointment for a period of eligibility as provided
by the rules of the board ;
(d ) For a period of probation not exceeding
twelve months nor less than six months before an
appointment or employment is made a regular
appointment ;
(e) For reinstatement on the eligible list of per-
sons who , without default or delinquency , are sep-
arated from the service ;
(f) For promotion from the lower grades to the
higher based on records of efficiency and seniority
to be furnished by the department , agency or com-
mission in which the person is employed and kept

CHAPTER 15 129
by said personnel commission , or upon competitive
promotion tests , or both .
§ 5. Appointments —Probationary , Regular and
Emergency . All appointments to the positions
subject to the provisions of this act shall be made
solely on the basis of merit and fitness , to be deter-
mined by competitive examinations , except in the
case of emergency appointments which may be
made only in accordance with the definition given
in Section 1 ; provided that all original appoint-
ments , except emergency appointments as defined
in Section 1 of this act , shall be probationary ap-
pointments for a period of not less than six nor
more than twelve months , after which probationary
period regular appointments shall be given to all
probationary employees who are deemed to be sat-
isfactory by their respective department , agency or
commission head .
Except in the case of emergency appointments
as defined in Section 1 of this act , all appointments
to positions subject to the provisions of this act
shall be made by appointing authorities only from
an eligible list certified by the personnel commis-
sioner as qualified and eligible for appointment from
an appropriate eligible list as defined in Section 1
of this act .
Dismissals or6. Dismissals and Demotions .
demotions of employees in positions subject to the
provisions of this act may be made only by the ap-
pointing authority upon the filing of written state-
ments of the reasons of such dismissals or demo-
tions with the personnel commission , provided that
copies of all such statements shall be furnished to
the employee so dismissed or demoted , on or before
the effective date of such dismissal or demotion ,
provided further that employees holding probation-

130 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ary appointments may be dismissed without the ap-
pointing authority being required to furnish either
the personnel commission or the dismissed or de-
moted employee with a written statement of the
reasons for such dismissal or demotion .
The personnel commission shall investigate and
determine the justification for all dismissals or de-
motions reported according to the provisions of
this section and shall make recommendations ac-
cording to its findings , approving or disapproving
the dismissal or demotion so reported . If the per-
sonnel commissioners disapprove any such dismis-
sal or demotion they shall recommend the reinstate-
ment or transfer of such dismissed or demoted em-
ployee . Copies of the recommendations of the per-
sonnel commissioners shall be given to the appoint-
ing authority making the dismissal or demotion and
to the dismissed or demoted employee .
The appointing authority receiving such a recom-
mendation shall have the option of either accepting
or rejecting such recommendation , providing that
all such recommendations made by the personnel
commissioners shall be public records , and provid-
ing further that the personnel commissioners may
reinstate a dismissed or demoted employee over the
action of the appointing authority only in case it
appears after a proper investigation that the dis-
missal or demotion was made for religious or pol-
itical reasons .
The personnel commission , in investigating the
dismissal or demotion of employees subject to the
provisions of this act , shall have the power to sub-
poena and require attendance of witnesses to give
testimony and to administer oaths to such
witnesses .
Nothing in this act shall permit the dismissal or
demotion of any employee subject to the provisions

CHAPTER 15 131
of this act on account of religious faith or political
affiliation.
§ 7. Other Functions of the Personnel Commis-
sion . The personnel commission shall perform
such other functions as may relate to the efficiency
of the personnel under its jurisdiction , including
the maintenance of the personnel classification plan
and the administration of service ratings , attend-
ance record , training programs , promotions , trans-
fers , layoffs , suspensions , reinstatements and re-
lated activities . The personnel commissioners shall
prepare appropriate rules for the administration
of these functions , and shall make an annual report
to the Mayor of activities of the personnel commis-
sion together with recommendations concerning
personnel policies , which report shall constitute
and be a public record open to inspection by inter-
ested citizens and taxpayers .
§ 8. Extension of Act to Other Positions , De-
partments or Agencies . The provisions of this act
may be extended by city ordinance to include addi-
tional positions or classes of positions in the De-
partment of Health and the Department of Welfare
and in any other departments of cities of the first
class except the Divisions of Police and Fire in the
Department of Safety . And it is further provided
that any independent agency of the city govern-
ment may adopt the provisions of this act for such
positions or classes of positions in such agency as
the governing body of such agency may designate ,
and when the provisions hereof shall have been so
extended by city ordinance or by action of the gov-
erning body of any independent agency then all of
the provisions of this act shall be applied to such
positions in such departments or agencies the same
as if originally included herein ; the effective date
of any extension of the provisions of this act to in-

132 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
clude additional positions or classes of positions
shall be considered as the effective date of this act
for such positions or classes of positions .
Employees holding positions subject to the pro-
visions of this act on the effective date hereof shall
be considered as having satisfied all of the qualifi-
cations for obtaining original appointments under
this act and shall be considered as having been given
probationary appointments as defined in Section 1
of the act as of the effective date of the act .
§ 9. Prohibition of and Penalty for Political Ac-
tivity and Contributions . No person in the compe-
titive service shall directly or indirectly solicit or
remit funds or in any manner be concerned with the
soliciting for or remitting funds to any political
party or any candidate for public office ; nor shall
any person take any active part in political cam-
paigns or be a member of any campaign commit-
tees or governing committees of any political or-
ganization . Nothing herein contained , however ,
shall be construed so as to prevent the personnel
commission from making more stringent rules and
regulations relative to political activity or partici-
pation on the part of the employees subject to the
provisions of this act .
Any violation of this section shall operate to for-
feit the office or position held by the person violat-
ing the same and shall render any such person in-
eligible to any municipal office or position in said
city coming under the provisions of this act for a
period of one year .
§ 10. Penalties . Any person who shall will-
fully, knowingly or through culpable negligence
violate any of the provisions of this act , shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor and shall upon conviction
thereof be subject to a fine of not less than fifty dol-

CHAPTER 16 133
lars nor more than five hundred dollars ; provided
that the provisions of this section shall not apply
to Section 9 of this act .
§ 11. Appropriations . The legislative body of
each such city of the first class shall pass such ordi-
nances , levy such taxes and provide such appropri-
ations as are necessary to make this act effective .
$ 12. Validity . Should any section , paragraph ,
sentence , clause or phrase of this act be declared
unconstitutional or invalid for any reason , the re-
mainder of said act shall not be affected thereby .
§ 13. Date of Effect . The act shall become ef-
fective as soon as possible after its passage and ap-
proval , but in no event later than September 1 ,
1937 .
Approved January 18 , 1937 .
CHAPTER 16 .
AN ACT to prohibit distribution , sale or exchange in Kentucky
of goods , wares or merchandise , manufactured in whole or in
part by convicts or prisoners , except those manufactured for
the use of the State in its institutions and administrative
branches of its government .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That from and after the effective date of this
Act , no goods , wares , or merchandise , manufac-
tured or produced wholly or in part by convicts or
prisoners ( except convicts or prisoners on parole
or probation ) in the State of Kentucky , or in any
state or district , of the United States , shall be sold
or offered for sale in this State , by any person ,
firm , association or corporation , or by any Federal
authority , state or political subdivision thereof ,
provided , however , that nothing in this Act shall be
construed to forbid the sale , exchange , or distribu-

134 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tion of such goods , to any institution supported
wholly by funds derived from public taxation , and
operated under the supervision of the United States
of America , the State of Kentucky , or any other
State of the Union , or any political subdivision
thereof , or to forbid the State of Kentucky from
manufacturing automobile tags , road signs , and
any other goods that said State has to furnish to
any of its institutions or to any branches of its gov-
ernment in its administrative capacity .
Any person , firm or corporation violating the
provisions of this Act , shall upon conviction
thereof, be fined not exceeding $ 1,000.00 , for each
separate offense , or by imprisonment for not more
than six months , or both so fined and imprisoned
in the discretion of the jury or court trying the
case .
All laws in conflict herewith are hereby repealed
and Sections 524 , 525 , and 526 of the 1930 Edition
of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , are hereby ex-
pressly repealed .
Approved January 18 , 1937 .

eral, acting ex officio , shall constitute the Kentucky

136 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
2. The Kentucky Real Estate Board shall

porate agency of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,

gether with the time and place of such sale . Said

methods of breaking packages and containers and
the methods of affixing the stamps shall be pre-
scribed by the Department in such a way as to en-
force the provisions of this measure . Be and the
same is hereby repealed , amended and reenacted so

follows :
" "
"The Department is hereby authorized and
directed to prescribe the forms of the stamps herein

142 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
provided for . It shall have such stamps prepared

saler , holding a valid permit from the Department
of Revenue and required by this Act and the regula-
tions issued pursuant thereto to purchase and affix

partment and actually affixed by said person , an

and the methods of affixing stamps shall be pre-
scribed by the Department in such a way as to

(4 ) of the Acts of the Third Extraordinary Session

of the 1936 General Assembly being entitled " An

CHAPTER 20.
AN ACT authorizing refund of taxes paid under provisions of
Chapter one hundred forty -nine ( 149 ) of the Acts of the Gen
eral Assembly of one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) by
the Auditor of Public Accounts upon the filing of claims and
providing that suits may be maintained upon the Auditor of
Public Accounts ' refusal to issue warrants when the claims
are properly filed ; prescribing the venue and jurisdiction for
the filing of such claims and for bringing of such suits ; the
manner in which they may be prosecuted , and waiving any
defenses which the Commonwealth might assert on account of
limitations , and on account of any failure to pay said taxes
under protest , and making an appropriation therefor .
Whereas , The General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred
thirty ( 1930 ) passed an Act which was approved by the Gov-
ernor on March seventeen ( 17 ) , one thousand nine hundred
thirty ( 1930 ) , which is compiled as Chapter one hundred
forty -nine ( 149 ) of the Acts of the General Assembly of one
thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) , imposing an excise or
license tax on retail merchants and prescribing heavy penal-
ties for the failure to report and pay the taxes therein im-
posed ; and
Whereas , The principal purpose of said Act was to tax chain
stores ; and
Whereas , The large chain stores and a number of other
large stores filed suits in the Federal Courts to enjoin and
did enjoin the State from collecting said taxes ; and
Whereas , In the case of the Kroger Grocery and Baking
Co. , et al v. Lewis , et al, two hundred ninety -four ( 294 )
U. S. five hundred fifty ( 550 ) , fifty -five ( 55 ) S. Ct . five hun-
dred twenty-five ( 525 ) , the Supreme Court of the United
States held the aforesaid Act to be unconstitutional , and on
April twenty-nine ( 29 ) , one thousand nine hundred thirty -five
(1935 ) , overruled a petition for a rehearing filed on behalf of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky in said case ; and
Whereas , By reason of such decision and of the aforesaid
suits the large chain stores and other large merchants es
caped entirely the payment of said tax ; and
Whereas , Before said Act was held unconstitutional a large
number of merchants had paid said tax from year to year as
and when due to avoid the penalties in said Act ; and
Whereas , The total amount so paid into the State Treas-
ury for the five -year period from the effective date of the Act
until same was held unconstitutional amounts in the aggre-

dollars , fifty -six cents ( $309,136.56 ) ; and

thereof except within two years from the date said taxes
were paid , and then only if paid under protest ; and
Whereas , They are justly entitled to a refund of said
taxes , and the limitations imposed in said Act have barred
the recovery of the taxes paid during the first three years of
the operation of said Act unless waived by the General As-
sembly of Kentucky ; and
Whereas , If the State should fail to authorize a refund

having been held constitutional and valid by a three-
judge Federal Court in the case of Kroger Grocery
and Banking Company . , et al . v. Lewis , et al , seven
( 7 ) Fed . Sup . four hundred thirty - eight (438 ) , de-
cided December twenty ( 20 ) , one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty -three ( 1933 ) ; and later having been held
unconstitutional by the Supreme Court of the
United States on an appeal from the aforesaid de-
cision , finally decided by the Supreme Court on
March eleven ( 11 ) , one thousand nine hundred thir-
ty -five ( 1935 ) , it is deemed by the General Assembly
that the two -year limitation imposed by Section ten
( 10 ) of said Chapter one hundred forty - nine ( 149 )
of the Acts of the General Assembly of one thousand
nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) and the limitation im-
posed by Section one hundred sixty -three ( 163 ) ,
Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's one thousand nine
hundred thirty ( 1930 ) edition , should be and the
same are hereby waived and the limitations thereby
imposed shall not be pleaded as a defense and shall
not be a defense to any claim or suit for a refund
of the aforesaid taxes . Provided , however , the
waiver of the defenses of limitations herein pro-
vided shall only apply to claims and suits filed with-
in six months from the effective date of this Act . If
said appropriation is not sufficient , an additional
amount of fifty thousand dollars ( $ 50,000 ) is hereby
appropriated so as to liquidate the entire obligation
of this Commonwealth to said taxpayers .
§ 4. Since Chapter one hundred forty -nine ( 149 )
of the Acts of the General Assembly of one thousand
nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) has been declared uncon-
stitutional and since in equity each person who paid
taxes under the said Act should be reimbursed and
since suits for each refund would be necessarily ex-
pensive both in time and in money both to the plain-

CHAPTER 21 149
tiffs and to the Commonwealth , the requirements in
Section ten ( 10 ) of said Act that recovery may be
had only by a suit against the Auditor of Public Ac-
counts is hereby explicitly waived .
§ 5. If judgment be rendered for plaintiff in any
suit brought under the provisions of this Act , the
Auditor of Public Accounts upon presentation to
him of an attested copy thereof shall issue his war-
rant upon the State Treasurer in favor of the plain-
tiff for the amount of the judgment and when the
warrant is presented to the State Treasurer he shall
issue his check payable to the payee therein for the
amount of the warrant , and both the warrant and
the check shall be charged to the appropriation made
by the General Assembly of Kentucky at the second
special session of 1936 , as provided in Section 55 ,
Chapter 51 of said Act .
Approved January 18 , 1937 .

Statutes ; and repealing all laws in conflict with this Act to
the extent of such conflict , and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The Commissioner of Revenue hereby is em-
powered and authorized to act in the name of and in
behalf of the Commonwealth of Kentucky and in the
name of and in behalf of any and all counties and
school districts and other taxing districts in the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , excepting and exclud-

clusive , to institute any suit , action or proceeding
for the collection of delinquent taxes and the assess-

linquent taxes or the assessment of omitted prop-
erty for the Commonwealth of Kentucky and any
and all counties , school districts and other taxing
districts in the Commonwealth of Kentucky , except-
ing and and excluding cities of the First to Sixth

countants and attorneys to bring and prosecute all
such actions and proceedings , and to fix the salaries

expenses . The employment and compensation of
such agents , accountants and attorneys shall be ap-
proved by the Governor of the Commonwealth of

CHAPTER 22 .
AN ACT to authorize cities of the first class to construct law
school buildings for municipal universities where the ground
for such buildings has been conveyed to the city free of cost ,
lien and encumbrance , providing that cities of the first class
may issue bonds to defray the cost of such building . Provid-
ing that the building so erected may be leased to the munici-
pal university for a rental sufficient to pay the bonds , the in-
terest thereon , insurance , and all operating expenses of such
building . Providing that the bonds shall be secured only by
revenues derived from the rental of such building and by lien
upon the land and improvements . Creating a statutory mort-
gage lien upon such building . Providing that such bonds shall
not constitute an indebtedness of the city within the meaning
of the constitutional provisions or limitations . Providing for
the making of plans and specifications for such building and
the approval of the municipal university therefor ; providing
for the deposit and security of rentals received from such
building and the application of the same to the payment of
bonds issued to pay for the cost of the construction of the said
building . Providing when rentals sufficient to pay the cost of
the erection of the said building , all operating costs , interest
on the bonds , the city shall thereupon convey the premises and
improvements to such municipal university , and declaring an
emergency to exist
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That cities of the first class of the Com-
monwealth are hereby authorized and empowered
to establish and erect law school buildings for
municipal universities existing in such cities ,
together with necessary appurtenances thereto ,
under the provisions of this act , for the purpose of
supplying the municipal universities of such cities
adequate buildings necessary to carry out their cor-
porate duties and powers .
§ 2. The provisions of this Act shall be applica-
ble only where any such municipal university shall
have conveyed or caused to have been conveyed to

CHAPTER 22 167
such city , the ground necessary for the erection of
such law school building , which conveyance shall be
made without cost to such city and the land so con-
veyed shall be free of all liens and encumbrances .
$ 3. For the purpose of defraying the cost of
acquiring or constructing any such law school build-
ings and appurtenances thereto , any such city may
borrow money secured by lien on the revenues de-
rived from such building , and issue negotiable
bonds , provided no such bonds shall be issued unless
and until authorized by an ordinance specifying the
proposed undertaking , the amount of bonds to be
issued , and the maximum rate of interest such bonds
are to bear, which shall not be more than six
per cent ( 6 % ) per annum . Such ordinance shall
further provide that the proposed law school build-
ing and appurtenances thereto are to be constructed
or acquired pursuant to the provisions of this Act .
§ 4. All bonds under the provisions of this Act
may be issued bearing interest at a rate not exceed-
ing six per cent ( 6 % ) per annum , payable semi-
annually , and shall be executed in such manner and
be payable at such times not exceeding fifty ( 50 )
years from date thereof, and at such place or places
as the Board of Aldermen of such City shall
determine .
5. Any and all such bonds shall have and are
hereby declared to have , in the hands of bona fide
holders , all of the qualities of negotiable instru-
ments under the law merchant , and shall not be sub-
ject to taxation . In case any of the officers whose
signatures or counter - signatures appearing on the
bonds or coupons shall cease to be such officers
before delivery of such bonds , such signatures or
counter -signatures shall , nevertheless , be valid and
sufficient for all purposes , the same as if they had

168 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
remained in office until such delivery . Such bonds
shall be sold in such manner and upon such terms
as the Board of Aldermen shall deem best for the
best interest of such city . Such bonds when issued
shall be payable solely from the revenue funds de-
rived from such law school building , as provided in
Sections 9 and 10 hereof , and shall not constitute an
indebtedness of such city within the meaning of the
provisions or limitations of the Constitution of
Kentucky . It shall be plainly stated on the face of
such bond that same has been issued under the pro-
visions of this Act , and that it does not constitute
an indebtedness of such city within the meaning of
any constitutional provisions or limitations .
§ 6. All moneys received from any bonds issued
pursuant hereto shall be applied solely for the es-
tablishment or erection of such law school building
and necessary appurtenances thereto ; provided ,
such moneys may be used also to advance the pay-
ment of the interest on bonds during the first three
years following the date of such bonds . And there
shall be , and there is hereby , created a statutory
mortgage lien upon such law school building and
appurtenances to and in favor of the holders of said
bonds , and each of them , and to and in favor of the
holders of the coupons of said bonds .
§ 7. The law school buildings so erected , to-
gether with the appurtenances thereto , shall remain
subject to such statutory lien until the payment in
full of the principal and interest of the bonds . Any
holder of said bonds , or any of the coupons , may
either at law or in equity protect and enforce the
statutory mortgage lien hereby conferred and may
bring suit , action , mandamus or other proceedings
to enforce and compel performance of all duties re-
quired by this Act , including the making and collect-

CHAPTER 22 169
ing of sufficient rents , the segregation of the income
and revenue and the application thereof.
$ 8. If there be any default in the payment of
the principal or interest on any of said bonds , any
court having jurisdiction of the action may appoint
a receiver to administer said law school building on
behalf of the city , with power to charge and collect
rentals sufficient to provide for the payment of any
bonds or obligations outstanding against said law.
school building , and for the payment of the operat-
ing expenses , and apply the income and revenues in
conformity with this Act and the ordinance referred
to in Sections 9 and 10 hereof.
9. When any such city shall desire to construct
such law school building , the Board of Aldermen of
such city shall by ordinance cause plans and speci-
fications for the law school building to be con-
structed to be duly made and filed in the office of
the Clerk of the Board of Aldermen , which plans
and specifications shall give a full description of
the building to be constructed , the details thereof
and the manner of construction .
The plans and specifications of such building
shall be prepared by an architect selected by the
city and approved by the Board of Trustees of such
Municipal University , shall be submitted to the
Board of Trustees of such Municipal University for
approval , and if approved , and if such Board of
Trustees shall offer in writing to lease such build-
ing for a term of years from the time such building
shall be completed and ready for occupancy , with
the right and option in such lessee to extend the
term of such lease for a term of years from
the expiration of the original term of such
lease , and for a term of years from the expir-
ation of such extended term ofof such lease

170 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
until the original term of such lease shall have been.
extended for a total number of years not exceeding
fifty ( 50 ) years , at a rental which if paid for the
original term and for each of the full number of
years for which the term of said lease may be ex-
tended , will amortize the total cost of the erection
of said building and appurtenances , provide an ade-
quate maintenance fund , and in addition thereto a
sum sufficient to pay the cost of insuring the build-
ing erected against loss or damage by fire or wind-
storm or other calamity , in such sum as may be
agreed by the parties thereto , but in no event shall
such insurance be less than the value of the im-
provements , the Board of Aldermen shall by ordi-
nance authorize the issuance of bonds in the man-
ner and form as herein provided , sufficient to pay
the cost of constructing such building and shall
cause the Clerk of the Board of Aldermen to adver-
tise for bids , and thereafter the Board of Alder-
men by and through the Mayor of such city may
contract for construction of such building .
10. At or before the issuance of such bonds ,
the Board of Aldermen of such city shall, by ordi-
nance , set aside and pledge the income of such
building into a separate and special fund to be used
and applied in payment of the cost thereof and in
maintenance thereof . Said ordinance shall defi-
nitely fix and determine the amount of revenue
which shall be necessary and be set aside and ap-
plied for the payment of the principal and in-
terest of the bonds ; and the balance of such income
shall be set aside for the reasonable and proper
maintenance thereof , including a sufficient sum to
pay the costs of such insurance , as hereinabove pro-
vided . The rentals to be charged for the use of
such building shall be sufficient to provide for the
payment of interest upon all bonds and to create a

CHAPTER 22 171
sinking fund to pay the principal thereof as and
when the same becomes due , and to provide for the
maintenance thereof , including the cost of insuring
such building erected against loss or damage by fire
and windstorm or other calamity .
§ 11. If any surplus be accumulated in the main-
tenance fund which shall be equal to the cost of
maintaining such building during the remainder of
the calendar or fiscal year , as may be provided by
the ordinance hereinbefore required , and the cost
of maintaining and operating such building the suc-
ceeding like calendar or fiscal year , any excess over
such amount shall be transferred to the sinking
fund .
§ 12. Such city may issue refunding bonds
for the purpose of providing funds for the pay-
ment of any outstanding bonds , in accordance with
the procedure prescribed by this Act . Such refund-
ing bonds shall be secured to the same extent and
shall have the same source of payment as the bonds
which shall have been thereby refunded.
§ 13. Should the Board of Aldermen find that
the bonds authorized will be insufficient to accom-
plish the purpose desired , additional bonds may be
authorized and issued subject to the procedure as
herein required .
$ 14. Such city in acquiring any building under
the provisions of this Act may provide by ordinance
any such provision and stipulation for the admin-
istration of the income for the security of the bond-
holders as the Board of Aldermen of such city may
deem necessary : Provided that the sinking fund
which shall be provided by ordinance as hereinbe-
fore required shall be deposited in a depository se-
lected by such Board of Aldermen , which deposit

172 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
may , if required by the Board of Aldermen , be con-
tinuously secured by a pledge to the city of direct
obligations of the United States of America , exclu-
sive of accrued interest , at all times at least equal to
the balance on deposit in such account or in such
other manner acceptable to the purchaser or hold-
ers of such bonds . Such securities shall either be-
deposited with the city or be held by a trustee or
agent satisfactory to the Board of Aldermen of such
city . The Board of Aldermen may by ordinance re-
quire that said sinking fund be invested in direct
obligations of the United States of America .
§ 15. When the Board of Trustees of such Mu-
nicipal University has paid rent in the manner and
form as provided in this Act sufficient to amortize
the cost of the erection of such building and appur-
tenances , to maintain such building and pay the cost
of insurance , such city shall thereupon convey said
premises to such Municipal University and shall
transfer the balance , if any , remaining in the funds
herein provided to the account of such municipal
university .
§ 16. This Act shall be deemed to create an ad-
ditional and alternate method for the construction
and acquisition of law school buildings for munici-
pal universities by any city of the first class and
shall not be deemed to include , alter , amend , or
repeal any other statute . No proceeding shall be
required for the acquisition of any law school build-
ing hereunder or the issuance of bonds hereunder
except such as are prescribed by this Act , any pro-
vision in the general laws of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky or Charter of any City to the contrary
notwithstanding .
WHEREAS , cities of the class to which this Act is
applicable are now without adequate buildings for

CHAPTER 23 173
the purpose of conducting law schools , thus endan-
gering the health , safety , convenience and general
welfare of students in such schools in all cities of
the first class ,
Now , THEREFORE , an emergency is declared to
exist and this Act shall take effect immediately
upon its passage and approval .
Approved January 17 , 1937 .

CHAPTER 23 .
AN ACT to authorize cities of the Second Class to establish and
maintain Junior Colleges by levying a tax for school purposes
and by charging tuition fees in support thereof under the man-
agement and control of the Board of Education of such cities .
This Act shall be known as the " H. S. Swope Act ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. The Boards of Education of cities of the
second class are hereby authorized to establish or to
acquire by purchase , gift , or lease , or by lawful con-
veyance , municipal Junior Colleges for the purpose
of promoting public education as defined in this act ;
and for the support and maintenance thereof the
Board of Education of such city may request and
the general council or other legislative body of such
city shall then annually cause to be levied and col-
lected a tax of not less than five ( 5 ) nor more than
seven (7 ) cents on each One Hundred Dollars ( $ 100 )
worth of property subject to the taxation for city
purposes .
§ 2. A municipal junior college within the mean-
ing of this act is a college established or supported
in whole or in part by funds raised by municipal tax-
ation and controlled and administered by the Board
of Education of such city as a part of the public

174 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
school system and affording instruction in arts ,
sciences , and professions and conferring such cer-
tificates of attainments as may be authorized by
other like or similar institutions of learning above
high school grade or standing.
§ 3. Such levy of taxes shall be made at the
same time and in the same manner as other levies
for public school purposes , and the amount so levied
shall be placed to the credit of the Board of Educa-
tion fund upon completion of the assessment of
property for taxation , and paid as collected , sub-
ject to such discounts or penalties as is allowed on
other city taxes , by the Treasurer of the city to the
Treasurer of the Board of Education for the pur-
pose of establishing , acquiring , maintaining , and
operating said municipal junior college . The taxes
authorized by this act to be levied shall be construed
to be school taxes and to be levied for school pur-
poses and shall be in addition to all other municipal
taxes authorized and allowed by law to be used for
municipal purposes and shall be in addition to all
other taxes authorized and allowed by law to be
levied for school purposes .
4. The Board of Education of any second-
class city that may establish or acquire a municipal
junior college under the terms of this act may , for
the purpose of supplementing the tax provided in
this act , charge each pupil attending the municipal
junior college a tuition fee not to exceed One Hun-
dred Dollars ( $ 100 ) per annum . The tuition fee
shall be collected by the Treasurer of the Board
of Education and shall be placed to the credit of the
Board of Education College Fund , and shall be used
for maintaining and operating the municipal junior .
college .

CHAPTER 24 175
§ 5. A municipal junior college shall be entitled
to support under this act only when its principal
work is the maintenance of courses of instruction
in advance of the instruction maintained in high
schools under the control of the Board of Educa-
tion .
§ 6. The Board of Education of any second-
class city may set apart or appropriate any site ,
or school building , or part of a school building , and
grounds , not especially needed for general school
purposes , for the use of said municipal junior
colleges .
§ 7. An annual report and an audit of receipts
and disbursements of the Junior College Fund shall
be made by the Board of Education of the city.
§ 8. Nothing in this Act shall be construed to
affect cities that have established , and are now oper-
ating such colleges other than by Boards of Educa-
tion .
Approved by the Governor .
CHAPTER 24 .
AN ACT to protect trade -mark owners , distributors and the pub-
lic against injurious and uneconomic practices in the sale
and distribution of articles of standard quality under a trade-
mark, brand or name and defining unfair competition . This
Act shall be known as "The Wylie Fair Trades Act ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. No contract relating to the sale or resale of
a commodity which bears , or the label or content of
which bears , the trade -mark , brand or name of the
producer or owner of such commodity and which is
in fair and open competition with commodities of

176 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the same general class produced by others shall be
deemed in violation of any law of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky by reason of any of the following pro-
visions which may be contained in such contract :
( 1 ) That the buyer will not resell any such com-
modity except at the price stipulated by the vendor .
( 2 ) That the producer or vendee of a commodity
require upon the sale of such commodity to another ,
that such purchaser agree that he will not , in turn ,
resell except at the price stipulated by such pro-
ducer or vendee . Such provisions in any contract
shall be deemed to contain or imply conditions that
such commodity may be resold without reference
to such agreement in the following cases : (1) In
closing out the owner's stock for the purpose of dis-
continuing delivery of any such commodity ; pro-
vided , however , that such stock is first offered to
the manufacturer of such stock at the original in-
voice stock price , at least ten ( 10 ) days before such
stock shall be offered by sale to the public . ( 2 ) When
the goods are damaged or deteriorated in quality ,
and notice is given to the public thereof . ( 3 ) By
any officer acting under the orders of any court .
§ 2. Willfully and knowingly advertising , offer-
ing for sale or selling any commodity at less than
the price stipulated in any contract entered into pur-
suant to the provisions of Section One ( 1 ) of this
Act, whether the person so advertising , offering for
sale or selling is or is not a party to such agreement ,
is unfair competition and is actionable at the suit
of any person damaged thereby .
3. This act shall not apply to any contract or
agreement between producers or between whole-
salers or between retailers as to sale or resale prices .
§ 4. If any section , sentence or clause of this
Act shall for any reason be held invalid or uncon-

CHAPTER 25 177
stitutional , such decision shall not affect the validity
of the remaining parts hereof.
§ 5. This Act may be known and cited as the
"Fair Trade Act . '99
Approved January 18 , 1937 .

In addition to all other compensation herein pro-

pense , within the limits of this action , incurred by

ployee reasonable burial expenses of a person of

bridge and , Whereas , it is desired by Ohio County that per-
mission of the Secretary of War be given for the construction
and erection of said bridge at said place , and , Whereas , the
erection of said bridge will in no way obstruct or hinder navi-
gation on said river , Now
Be it Resolved by the Senate of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , the House of Representa-
tives concurring therein :
§ 1. That the Secretary of War in Washington ,
D. C. , as provided in Section eight hundred fifty-
three a- one ( 853a - 1 ) of the Kentucky Statutes ,
grant permission to Ohio County to construct and
relocate a highway bridge at Carters Ferry across
Rough River , and that the permission be granted to
erect said bridge at said place .
Approved by the Governor .
CHAPTER 29 .
WHEREAS , The present employees and officers of the General
Assembly have rendered faithful service in both day and night
sessions of the General Assembly , meeting in special session ,
during the year 1936-1937 , and
WHEREAS , The General Assembly, in view of the services ren-
dered , deems the compensation allowed by law as provided
for in Senate Bill 314 of the Acts of the General Assembly of
1936 , inadequate , Now Therefore
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 29 185
§ 1. That there is hereby appropriated from the
Legislative Session Fund as additional compensa-
tion , for the employees and officers of the Senate
and the House of Representatives , to that compen-
sation provided in Senate Bill No. 314 of the Acts
of the General Assembly of 1936 , the sum of
$8,659.00 .
2. That the Chief Clerk of the Senate shall
requisition and the Commissioner of Finance shall
approve , and the Auditor of Public Accounts shall
draw a warrant , for the sum of $4,446.00 on the
Treasury of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in fa-
vor of the Chief Clerk of the Senate . Such Chief
Clerk shall forthwith distribute to the employees
and officers of the Senate such funds on the basis
of the number of days that such employees and of-
ficers served during the special sessions of the Gen-
eral Assembly in the year 1936-1937 , the rate per
day to be computed by said Chief Clerk and the pre-
siding officer of the Senate on a pro rata basis of
the amounts authorized to be paid them by Senate
Bill No. 314 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of 1936 .
$ 3. The Chief Clerk of the House of Repre-
sentatives shall requisition and the Commissioner
of Finance shall approve , and the Auditor of public
Accounts shall draw a warrant , for the sum of
$4,213.00 on the Treasury of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky in favor of the Chief Clerk of the House
of Representatives . Such Chief Clerk shall forth-
with distribute to the employees and officers of the
House of Representatives such funds on the basis
of the number of days that such employees and of-
ficers served during the special sessions of the Gen-
eral Assembly in the year 1936-1937 , the rate per
day to be computed by said Chief Clerk and the pre-

186 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
siding officer of the House of Representatives on a
pro rata basis of the amounts authorized to be paid.
them by Senate Bill No. 314 of the Acts of the Gen-
eral Assembly of 1936 .
§ 4. All Acts or parts of Acts in conflict here-
with to the extent of such conflict are hereby
repealed .
§ 5. Whereas , the General Assembly will within
a short time adjourn and the employees and officers
will depart to various sections of the State , an
emergency is hereby declared to exist , and this
Resolution shall be effective from and after its pas-
sage and approval by the Governor of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky .
Approved by the Governor .
CHAPTER 30 .
A JOINT RESOLUTION ratifying the proposed amendment to
the Constitution of the United States to authorize Congress to
limit , regulate and prohibit the labor of persons under eighteen
(18 ) years of age . Whereas , both Houses of the Congress of
the United States of America by a constitutional majority of
two-thirds thereof made the following proposition to amend the
Constitution of the United States of America , in the following
words , to wit : "Joint Resolution , proposing to amend the
Constitution relating to the limiting , regulation and prohibition
of the labor of persons under eighteen ( 18 ) years of age :
Resolved by the Senate and House of Representatives of the
United States of America in Congress Assembled : Two-thirds
of each House concurring therein , that the following Article
is proposed as an amendment to the Constitution , which shall
be valid to all intents and purposes as part of the Constitution ,
when ratified by the Legislature of three -fourths of the several
states . Section One ( 1 ) . That Congress shall have power
to limit , regulate and prohibit the labor of persons under
eighteen ( 18 ) years of age . Section Two ( 2 ) . The power of
the several states is unimpaired by this Article , except that the

CHAPTER 31 187
>>
operation of the State laws shall be suspended to the extent
necessary to give effect to legislation enacted by the Congress
therefore ."
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the foregoing and above recited.
amendment to the Constitution of the United States
of America be and the same is hereby ratified by the
General Assembly of the Commonwealth ( State ) of
Kentucky as an amendment to the Constitution of
the United States .
2 . That the Governor of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky forthwith forward to the Secretary of
State of the United States an authenticated copy of
the foregoing Joint Resolution .
Approved by the Governor .
CHAPTER 31.
A RESOLUTION authorizing the General Assembly to appropri-
ate and pay to Dora Evans , the mother of Clarence Evans , the
per diem which would have been due and payable to Clarence
Evans during this Special Session of December twenty-three
( 23 ) , one thousand nine hundred thirty -six ( 1936 ) , to January
sixteen ( 16 ) , one thousand nine hundred thirty -seven ( 1937 ) ,
and declaring that said per diem is a necessary and legitimate
expense of this present Legislative session . Whereas , the Hon .
Clarence Evans of Franklin , Simpson County , Kentucky , was
duly elected a member of the Legislature from the twenty-
seventh ( 27th ) legislative district of Kentucky and that his
term of office as such member of the Legislature would not
have expired until December thirty -one ( 31 ) , one thousand nine
hundred thirty -seven ( 1937 ) , and Whereas , the said Clarence
Evans died while a member of the Lgislature from the said
twenty-seventh ( 27th ) legislative district of Kentucky on the
day of March , one thousand nine hundred thirty -six ( 1936 ) ,
and Whereas , no person has been elected or qualified to repre-
sent said district , and Whereas , the Governor of the Common--

wealth of Kentucky called the Legislature into special session
on December twenty-three ( 23 ) , one thousand nine hundred
thirty -six ( 1936 ) , and Whereas , by joint resolution of the
House and Senate the said General Assembly will adjourn on
January sixteen ( 16 ) , one thousand nine hundred thirty -seven
( 1937 ) , and Whereas , Dora Evans is the mother of Clarence
Evans and depended to a great extent for her support upon
her son , the deceased , Clarence Evans , and Whereas , the Gen-
eral Assembly has heretofore passed resolution appropriating
seventy -five thousand ( $ 75,000.00 ) dollars for the expenses of
this present special session of the Legislature and out of which
the per diem of each member of the Legislature will be paid ,
and Whereas , the Legislature feels that the per diem which
would have been due the said Clarence Evans if he had lived
and served his full term of office as a member of the General
Assembly as representative from the said twenty-seventh
( 27th ) legislative district of Kentucky , should be and is hereby
appropriated and allowed and ordered paid to his mother , Dora
Evans , and Whereas , it is resolved that same is legitimate ex-
pense of this present session of the Legislature , Therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the per diem due Clarence Evans as a mem-
ber of the Legislature from the twenty- seventh
(27th ) legislative district of Kentucky be now al-
lowed , appropriated and paid to Dora Evans , his
mother , and that the Auditor of Public Accounts be
and is hereby directed to draw a warrant for the
amount which would have been due Clarence Evans
during this special session of the Legislature in fa-
vor of Dora Evans , mother of Clarence Evans and
that the Treasurer draw his check for said amount
payable to Dora Evans as provided for in this
resolution . Approved by the Governor .
